reasoning: Ding Liren was ranked precisely #20 on the March 1, 2025 FIDE rating list with a rating of 2731. FIDE rankings are published on the 1st of every month, meaning his March 30 rating remains exactly #20, and 'as high or higher' requires a rank of 20 or better. Ding has not played any classical tournament chess in March 2025, which means his Elo is virtually guaranteed to stay 2731 for the April 1st list. Because there are several players clustered within a few points of him, he could drift up or down based entirely on the performances of others. However, without actively playing, maintaining his position is the likeliest outcome for next month (April 2025). The Wikipedia page typically updates slowly, so both the April 6 and April 29 dates will resolve to the April ranking (or the safely retained March ranking if Wikipedia is delayed), resulting in a solid >50% probability he stays <=20. As the forecast horizon extends to June, September, and eventually March  2026, his recent performance level (~2680-2700 strength) heavily indicates he will likely bleed rating whenever he elects to play, or continuously drop in position as active players secure positive results. As required, my forecast reflects increasing uncertainty on later dates by compressing closer to 0.5.
